category_title: Students who interject on-topic comments to avoid reading text
minimal_context: The student and tutor are looking at a page with pictures of monsters during a shared reading session of 'Where the wild things are'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is the next page with the monsters. Pretty scary.

STUDENT: (Looking at the picture of the monsters) I'll kill it.

TUTOR: Okay. What else?

STUDENT: I'll kill them.

TUTOR: Let's try to read the words on the page now.

STUDENT: I'll, I'll stab it in their eyes.

options.A: Provide written feedback using a 3-point Likert-type scale ranging from 1 (needs improvement) to 3 (great job) on the student's daily reading progress report for the session.
options.B: Offer a short synopsis and a direct question related to the text (e.g., 'Max shows up suddenly on this island. I think they're trying to scare him away maybe... what kinds of things did they do to scare him away?').
options.C: Respond with enthusiastic praise for the student's comments about the pictures (e.g., 'Another one already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going.').
options.D: Provide the correct articulation of a specific word on the page (e.g., 'Monsters') to prompt the student to read during the shared reading session.
